title: Press

bodyBlocks.label: ECM Reviews, NYC
bodyBlocks.content:
> “Clang Sayne craft a tender yet restless atmosphere, revealing a lifetime’s worth of impressions with dirge-like pathos.”

[December 30th 2018: KLAENG Festival live review](https://ecmreviews.com/2018/12/30/live-report-klaeng-festival-2018/?fbclid=IwAR0_Ch9x42PcUk6j04q-6VY7t-kJAtEYLfoZ5m-IeVUygzTVHUA8_jmcn_Q/) — ECM Reviews, NYC

bodyBlocks.label: Irish Times, December 2018
bodyBlocks.content:
> “musical alchemist Laura Hyland is an explorer of the hidden paths and less-travelled byways that connect folk and traditional music with contemporary jazz and the avant-garde.”

[December 15th 2018: 'Sounding Seams' Preview](https://www.irishtimes.com/culture/music/forging-some-fine-sounds-this-week-s-best-jazz-gigs-1.3732003/) — Cormac Larkin, Irish Times

bodyBlocks.label: Irish Times, August 2018
bodyBlocks.content:
> “venturers in the post-genre slip stream, weaving folk, improv, art pop and contemporary classical into new material that is soft on the ear but tough as leather.”

[August 4th 2018: 'Summer Tour Preview'](https://www.irishtimes.com/culture/music/and-the-beat-goes-on-the-week-s-best-jazz-gigs-1.3585933/) — Cormac Larkin, Irish Times

bodyBlocks.label: Journal of Music
bodyBlocks.content:
> “The band draws a remarkable line between the folk aesthetic of the 60s/70s and the new music strands of today, perhaps in the search for a new, more direct form of musical expression.”

[Ergodos Santa Rita Series Live review](http://journalofmusic.com/criticism/journey/) — Anna Murray, Journal of Music, October 23rd 2017

bodyBlocks.label: Fractured Air, July 2017
bodyBlocks.content:
> “The Round Soul Of The World is a stunning achievement in distilling myriad themes (chiefly those of mortality, death and love) in a quietly breathtaking and poignant way.”

[Fractured Air Interview, July 8th 2017](https://fracturedair.com/2017/08/08/central-and-remote-clang-sayne/)

bodyBlocks.label: The Ticket
bodyBlocks.content:
> “Hyland’s soundscapes explore the rugged uplands criss-crossed by contemporary folk, avant-pop, improvisation and acoustic abstraction. A singular, defiantly independent voice in the lineage of Joni Mitchell, Kate Bush, and Bjork”

['Round Soul of the World' Review](https://fracturedair.com/2017/08/08/central-and-remote-clang-sayne/) — The Ticket, March 17th 2017

bodyBlocks.label: Irish Times, March 2017
bodyBlocks.content:
> “the hypnotic, hymnal nature of these songs fuels a desire to unravel their mysteries.”

["Round Soul of the World" Review](https://www.irishtimes.com/culture/music/clang-sayne-the-round-soul-of-the-world-album-review-hypnotic-in-its-nonconformity-1.3013382/) — Lauren Murphy, The Irish Times, March 23rd 2017

bodyBlocks.label: On the Record
bodyBlocks.content:
> “full of freestyle guile, wonky pop spirit and avant-everything experimental folk-jazz moxie.”

["Curse You Mocking Moon" Single Review](https://fracturedair.com/2017/08/08/central-and-remote-clang-sayne/) — Jim Carroll, On the Record, October 2016

bodyBlocks.label: No More Workhorse
bodyBlocks.content:
> “One of the most interesting new bands from Ireland in recent times.”

["Round Soul of the World" review](https://nomoreworkhorse.com/2017/03/21/new-music-clang-sayne-curse-you-mocking-moon/No More Workhorse Blog/) — March 2017

bodyBlocks.label: The Thin Air
bodyBlocks.content:
> “a sonic landscape of real, oftentimes transcendental beauty. Sparse in nature yet refined in its poise and glow, the album is a consistently intriguing extended tale in experimental folk”

["Round Soul of the World" review](http://thethinair.net/2017/03/album-premiere-clang-sayne-the-round-soul-of-the-world/) — The Thin Air Magazine, March 21st 2017

bodyBlocks.label: The Wire
bodyBlocks.content:
> “This is a set of live, organic, no-safety-net performances; there is something exhilarating about this modest yet perverse album and its refusal to conform.”

['Winterlands' Review](#) — Clive Bell, The Wire, June 2010

bodyBlocks.label: Mapsidaisical
bodyBlocks.content:
> “The raw no-overdubs recording style adds a sense of fluidity to the album, a real sense that on another day the band's improvisational compass could have steered these songs to different waters altogether.”

['Winterlands' Review](#) — Scott McMillian, Mapsidaisical, April 2010

bodyBlocks.label: Tokafi
bodyBlocks.content:
> “A brittly beautiful collection of autumnal songs - partly structured, partly free-floating, these pieces feel both weightless and airy as well as astoundingly clearly shaped.”

Tokafi interview / review

bodyBlocks.label: Music Machine
bodyBlocks.content:
> “The milky artwork and packaging bring to mind a pearl, one that stands out all the more from so many other gaudy synthetic gems.”

Serdar Yegulalp, Music Machine

bodyBlocks.label: Baked Goods
bodyBlocks.content:
> “This is an album that could have been recorded at any point between 1969 and the present day, music that transcends the background of its contributors or any expectation a listener might bring.”

Baked Goods

bodyBlocks.label: Boomkat
bodyBlocks.content:
> “The quartet's stunning [song] ‘A Death And A Vision’ showcases an uncategorisable approach to song craft that brings to mind the music of Tim Buckley, Jandek and David Sylvian's collaborations with Derek Bailey.”

Boomkat

bodyBlocks.label: Organ Art
bodyBlocks.content:
> “Improvisation yes, but the songs seem to have some kind of logical structure, a frame holding things in place. Mysteriously inviting, with an almost Siren like vibrancy; this is an album of intriguing beauty that's well worth your time.”

Organ Art

bodyBlocks.label: William Stafford Estate
bodyBlocks.content:
> “I found [your setting of 'A Ritual To Read To Each Other'] memorable and haunting, a very intriguing account of the poem, and very suitable.”

Response from The William Stafford Estate

bodyHtml:
“Clang Sayne craft a tender yet restless atmosphere, revealing a lifetime’s worth of impressions with dirge-like pathos. "

December 30th 2018: KLAENG Festival live review: ECM Reviews, NYC.

"musical alchemist Laura Hyland is an explorer of the hidden paths and less-travelled byways that connect folk and traditional music with contemporary jazz and the avant-garde."

December 15th 2018: 'Sounding Seams' Preview - Cormac Larkin, Irish Times, Irl

"venturers in the post-genre slip stream, weaving folk, improv, art pop and contemporary classical into new material that is soft on the ear but tough as leather."

August 4th 2018: 'Summer Tour Preview - Cormac Larkin, Irish Times, Irl.

“The band draws a remarkable line between the folk aesthetic of the 60s/70s and the new music strands of today, perhaps in the search for a new, more direct form of musical expression. "

Ergodos Santa Rita Series Live review : Anna Murray, Journal of Music, Irl, Oct 23rd 2017

“The Round Soul Of The World” is a stunning achievement in distilling myriad themes (chiefly those of mortality, death and love) in a quietly breathtaking and poignant way. "

Fractured Air Interview, July 8th 2017

"Hyland’s soundscapes explore the rugged uplands criss-crossed by contemporary folk, avant-pop, improvisation and acoustic abstraction. A singular, defiantly independent voice in the lineage of Joni Mitchell, Kate Bush, and Bjork"

'Round Soul of the World' Review: The Ticket, March 17th, 2017

"the hypnotic, hymnal nature of these songs fuels a desire to unravel their mysteries."

"Round Soul of the World" Review: Lauren Murphy, The Irish Times, March 23rd 2017

"full of freestyle guile, wonky pop spirit and avant-everything experimental folk-jazz moxie."

"Curse You Mocking Moon" Single Review: Jim Carroll, On the Record, October 2016

"One of the most interesting new bands from Ireland in recent times."

"Round Soul of the World" review: No More Workhorse Blog, March 2017

"a sonic landscape of real, oftentimes transcendental beauty. Sparse in nature yet refined in its poise and glow, the album is a consistently intriguing extended tale in experimental folk"

"Round Soul of the World" review: The Thin Air Magazine, March 21st, 2017

"This is a set of live, organic, no-safety-net performances; there is something exhilarating about this modest yet perverse album and its refusal to conform."

'Winterlands' Review: Clive Bell, The Wire, June 2010

"The raw no-overdubs recording style adds a sense of fluidity to the album, a real sense that on another day the band's improvisational compass could have steered these songs to different waters altogether"

'Winterlands' Review: Scott McMillian, Mapsidaisical, April 2010

"A brittly beautiful collection of autumnal songs - partly structured, partly free-floating, these pieces feel both weightless and airy as well as astoundingly clearly shaped."

Tokafi interview / review

"The milky artwork and packaging bring to mind a pearl, one that stands out all the more from so many other gaudy synthetic gems."

Serdar Yegulalp, Music Machine

"This is an album that could have been recorded at any point between 1969 and the present day, music that transcends the background of its contributors or any expectation a listener might bring."

Baked Goods

"The quartet's stunning [song] "A Death And A Vision" showcases an uncategorisable approach to song craft that brings to mind the music of Tim Buckley, Jandek and David Sylvian's collaborations with Derek Bailey."

Boomkat

"Improvisation yes, but the songs seem to have some kind of logical structure, a frame holding things in place. Mysteriously inviting, with an almost Siren like vibrancy; this is an album of intriguing beauty that's well worth your time."

Organ Art

"I found [your setting of 'A Ritual To Read To Each Other'] memorable and haunting, a very intriguing account of the poem, and very suitable."

Response from The William Stafford Estate
